title: Coimbatore Export Surge: Engineering Goods Hit ₹18,450 Crore
summary: Engineering goods and textiles exports from Coimbatore reached record highs in FY2026, recording 12.3% growth—highest in a decade. Industry credits improved port connectivity and bilateral trade agreements.

title: Smart Traffic System Reduces Congestion by 28% on Avinashi Road
summary: AI-powered traffic management system launched on Coimbatore's busiest 12-km arterial. Real-time vehicle counting and adaptive signals optimize flow during peak hours.

title: Rail Traffic Increases Ahead of Polling
summary: Railway stations recorded higher footfall as voters traveled to participate in elections.

title: Heat Levels Rise Across City
summary: Temperatures remained above average, prompting precautionary measures at polling booths.

title: Mobile Medical Units Activated
summary: Health teams deployed across key locations to respond to heat-related emergencies.

summary: Water points and sanitation facilities were set up to improve voter comfort.

summary: First-time voters expressed enthusiasm and participated actively in awareness drives.

summary: Many schools prepared to host voting booths.

summary: Price fluctuations observed due to supply shifts.

summary: Authorities activated helplines for voter assistance.

summary: Strict safeguards implemented to protect voting machines.

summary: Online portals enabled for voter complaints and queries.

summary: Ramps and access paths created for differently-abled voters.

summary: Markets saw higher activity during cooler hours.

title: Coimbatore Hottest Campaign Zone as Party Heavyweights Crowd City
summary: Prime Minister Modi and CM Stalin's visits drew parallel crowds as Coimbatore emerges as the state's central campaign battleground. Security was tightened across key corridors reflecting the district's importance in the 2026 race.

title: AIADMK Sweeping Comeback Projected from Kongu Belt
summary: Former minister S.P. Velumani predicted AIADMK-led alliance victory with Edappadi K. Palaniswami as chief minister. The party's confidence underlines how Kongu districts are being treated as the decisive battleground.

title: DMK Unveils ₹1 Lakh Crore Development Package for Coimbatore
summary: V. Senthilbalaji unveiled an exclusive manifesto promising projects worth ₹1 lakh crore over five years. The focus is on industrial voters seeking infrastructure, transport, and industrial ease.

title: Bus Shortage Triggers Angry Commuter Protests Before Polling
summary: Hundreds of passengers blocked the road at Singanallur terminus complaining about too few government buses. The episode exposed how election travel pressure can turn into civic grievance when services don't match demand.

title: Sari-Clad Robot Welcomes Voters at Coimbatore Booths
summary: Coimbatore polling stations tried unusual voter-engagement tactics with robots and plantable return gifts. The city's election-day style has become a talking point, mixing technology with voter outreach.

title: Coimbatore Crosses 38.62% Turnout by 11 AM
summary: Coimbatore district showed highly charged polling environment with repeated voter movement. The numbers are being read as a sign of sustained interest from the politically restless city.

title: Election Officials Step Up Checks Across Region
summary: GPS installed in EVM vehicles and flying squads made cash and material seizures. The message from the administration is clear: the region is being watched closely for money-driven violations.

title: Rising Costs Squeeze Railway and Engineering Suppliers
summary: MSME vendors serving railways asked for urgent relief as raw material prices rise and margins narrow. The concern matters far beyond one sector because Coimbatore's small-industrial economy is deeply tied to supply-chain stability.

title: Coimbatore MSME Ecosystem Faces Liquid-Fuel Crunch
summary: Nearly 30% of micro, small and medium units reportedly shut down due to LPG shortage. In a city that sells itself as Tamil Nadu's industrial engine, this became a symbol of how fragile production can be.

title: Coimbatore Exporters Watching Election Through Logistics Lens
summary: Local reporting ties voter mood to industrial issues like LPG availability, parking charges, transport delays. For business owners, the campaign is less about ideology and more about reducing friction in operations.

title: Hailstorm Damage Leaves Farmers Counting Losses
summary: Reports from Annur and nearby belts show thousands of acres hit with banana, coconut, and watermelon crops damaged at harvest stage. Farmers are pushing for assessment and compensation before the season slips further.

title: Coimbatore Airport Expansion Becomes Campaign Battleground
summary: Union Minister L. Murugan accused DMK government of delaying the project by five years. The airport issue sits at the intersection of business growth, regional pride, and electoral accountability.

title: Modi's Campaign Visit Tests Coimbatore's Transport Capacity
summary: Authorities deployed 2,600 police personnel and prepared for a crowd exceeding two lakh at CODISSIA Grounds. The scale shows how seriously all sides view the city as an infrastructure-and-politics showcase.

title: Rail Connectivity Remains Major Talking Point Across Kongu
summary: Calls for new Podanur-Karur line and special services show the region's transport appetite. Business leaders want rail capacity to keep pace with industrial and passenger growth.

title: Commuters Continue Complaining About Railway-Side Parking Costs
summary: Private contractor's higher parking charges at Coimbatore Junction drew attention as yet another persistent urban grievance. These issues shape the city's everyday opinion of governance.

title: Bus and Road Disruptions Become Recurring Election-Season Story
summary: Transport shortfalls affect long-distance voters trying to return home. In a region with major inter-district movement, transport failures are political failures too.

title: Special Trains Continue to Matter Due to High Migration
summary: Recent rail notices for Podanur and Coimbatore underline heavy movement of workers between industrial towns and other states. Transport policy is also labour policy in practice.

title: Road Safety Remains Hidden Infrastructure Crisis
summary: Krishnagiri crash and Valparai ghat accidents remind voters that highways still carry high human cost. Better enforcement and road engineering are becoming campaign expectations.

title: Coimbatore Student Ecosystem Folded Into Election Narrative
summary: Sari-clad robot at Government Arts College and youth turnout show colleges are part of the city's democratic branding. Local leaders use youth-facing language as social-media politics proves insufficient.

title: Coimbatore Job Market Shaped by Small-Industry Stress
summary: MSME shutdowns linked to LPG shortages and rising costs hit engineering, export, and ancillary jobs. The city's youth watch whether politicians offer practical employment relief or only campaign language.

title: Coimbatore Central Prison Spotlight After Inmate Violence
summary: Report of prisoners assaulting another inmate added to city's law-and-order concerns. Prison management is becoming a visible political issue in urban Tamil Nadu.

title: Coimbatore Police Battle Serious Violent Crime Cases
summary: Recent news includes acid attack and immolation attempt cases. These incidents have fed perception that the city's crime profile is becoming more volatile and harder to predict.

title: Law-Enforcement Agencies Tracking Alcohol and Election-Linked Offences
summary: District has seen liquor seizures, campaign violations, and heightened crowd control around polling stations. High-stakes urban election spills into criminal complaints quickly.

title: 107-Year-Old Farmer Votes for 72nd Time
summary: Despite age-related difficulties, farmer reached polling station with family help. His participation became one of the most memorable health-and-democracy moments of the day.

title: Coimbatore Hospitals Face Safety and Treatment Challenges
summary: A student drowning on university campus, prison-related assault, and serious injuries kept emergency response in the public eye. Residents increasingly ask if urban health systems are prepared for crisis load.

title: Dump-Yard and Waste Issues Remain Environmental Flashpoint
summary: Fires and animal-waste complaints at Vellalore repeatedly draw attention to city's waste-management gaps. Residents see these as not just sanitation failures but as long-term public-health hazards.

title: Weather-Linked Crop Damage Feeding Compensation Debate
summary: Hailstorm destroyed crops exactly when summer prices should have helped recover costs. Situation now being discussed alongside government relief and climate resilience planning.

title: Coimbatore Public Mood Mix of Civic Pride and Frustration
summary: City celebrating technical voter engagement and high-profile rallies yet grappling with transport shortages, police clashes, and industrial stress. That contradiction makes it a politically important urban battleground.
